You should migrate to Amazon ElastiCache for Valkey as noted in the [caches-for-service-data](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/technology-stays-current/caches-for-service-data/) production standard.

- BackupTagMissingInCriticalDataStoreRule
- CriticalTagMissingInOperationalDataStoreRule
- EC2SecurityGroupOpenToWorldRule
- ProjectTagInSelfServeRule

Description:
This rule is evaluated at the project level. Projects with caches must use Amazon ElastiCache
for Redis or Valkey. Memcached and non-Valkey ElastiCache Serverless deployments are not
permitted, as per
[technology-stays-current/caches-for-service-data](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/technology-stays-current/caches-for-service-data/).

Your project has one or more caches using a non-compliant engine (Memcached or ElastiCache Serverless).
You should migrate to Amazon ElastiCache for Valkey as noted in the [caches-for-service-data](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/technology-stays-current/caches-for-service-data/) production standard.

Rule: Projects must not use end-of-life versions of programming languages

Description: Projects with CloudFormation stacks must have CFRipper security scanning data available, as per [customer-data-stays-protected/cloudformation-scanning](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/customer-data-stays-protected/cloudformation-scanning/).

Please check the status of your CloudFormation stacks to ensure they are in a healthy state. Stacks in statuses like REVIEW_IN_PROGRESS or DELETE_FAILED may prevent template retrieval for security scanning, block resource updates, or lead to unexpected costs. If you believe your stacks should have been scanned and this does not apply to you, please contact Shield squad in #security-support.

More info about CFRipper can be found [here](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/SEC/pages/172832623/CFRipper).

Description: AI agents, batch jobs, and ETL pipelines must use Python as the primary programming language, as per [technology-stays-current/batch-job](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/technology-stays-current/batch-job/).

Rule: Repositories must use Docker base images from ECR
Description: Repositories must use Docker base images from the internal ECR repository, not from the public internet, as per [technology-stays-current/container-images](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/technology-stays-current/container-images/).

Rule: Repositories must use supported Docker images
Description:
Repositories must use official Skyscanner [Docker images](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/l/cp/CCfXdwKF) for all supported languages which include performance and security enhancements, as per
[technology-stays-current/container-images](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/technology-stays-current/container-images/).
Using stale or unsupported Docker images exposes Skyscanner travelers to security threats.

Please use official Skyscanner Docker images for all your projects. Review and act upon any open Dependabot pull requests against your repositories. If your project cannot receive dependency bumps by Dependabot, update your Docker images to the most recent version and reach out for support.

Rule: Web codebases must not use AWS SDK JS v2 (EOL 08/09/25)

- The current [skyscanner standard](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/technology-stays-current/aws-sdk-js/) requires the **v3 @aws-sdk** library to be used instead

  - **v3** is referenced as **`'@aws-sdk/*'`**

Description: Projects must not have critical CFRipper issues related to backup tags, security groups, and project tags across any of their CloudFormation stacks, as per [customer-data-stays-protected/cloudformation-scanning](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/customer-data-stays-protected/cloudformation-scanning/).
Failure Message:
Please remediate the following CFRipper issues across all CloudFormation stacks in this project, which are considered particularly important by the Security team:
- BackupTagMissingInCriticalDataStoreRule
- CriticalTagMissingInOperationalDataStoreRule
- EC2SecurityGroupOpenToWorldRule
- ProjectTagInSelfServeRule
- ProjectTagPresentRule
- RestoreInCriticalDataStoreNonCompliantRule

You can view the specific findings in the [custom data section](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/SEC/pages/172832635/CFRipper+FAQ#Where-can-I-see-my-stack-issues-in-Cortex%3F).

If you believe an exception is required, please submit a request in #security-support with the [necessary information](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/SEC/pages/172832635/CFRipper+FAQ#How-can-I-get-an-exception-for-my-stack%3F).

More details about CFRipper can be found [here](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/SEC/pages/172832623/CFRipper).

Rule: Repositories must use supported Docker images
Description:
Repositories must use official Skyscanner [Docker images](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/l/cp/CCfXdwKF) for all supported languages which include performance and security enhancements, as per
[technology-stays-current/container-images](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/technology-stays-current/container-images/).
Using stale or unsupported Docker images exposes Skyscanner travelers to security threats.

Please use official Skyscanner Docker images for all your projects. Review and act upon any open Dependabot pull requests against your repositories. If your project cannot receive dependency bumps by Dependabot, update your Docker images to the most recent version and reach out for support.

Rule: Projects must have no CFRipper selected issues across all stacks
Description: Projects must not have critical CFRipper issues related to backup tags, security groups, and project tags across any of their CloudFormation stacks, as per [customer-data-stays-protected/cloudformation-scanning](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/customer-data-stays-protected/cloudformation-scanning/).
Failure Message:
Please remediate the following CFRipper issues across all CloudFormation stacks in this project, which are considered particularly important by the Security team:
- BackupTagMissingInCriticalDataStoreRule
- CriticalTagMissingInOperationalDataStoreRule
- EC2SecurityGroupOpenToWorldRule
- ProjectTagInSelfServeRule
- ProjectTagPresentRule
- RestoreInCriticalDataStoreNonCompliantRule

You can view the specific findings in the [custom data section](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/SEC/pages/172832635/CFRipper+FAQ#Where-can-I-see-my-stack-issues-in-Cortex%3F).

If you believe an exception is required, please submit a request in #security-support with the [necessary information](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/SEC/pages/172832635/CFRipper+FAQ#How-can-I-get-an-exception-for-my-stack%3F).

More details about CFRipper can be found [here](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/SEC/pages/172832623/CFRipper).

Rule: Repositories must have all package.json files appropriately locked
Description: Repositories must have all package.json files properly locked to ensure reproducible builds, as per [changes-ship-reliably/reproducible-builds](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/changes-ship-reliably/reproducible-builds/).
Failure Message:
Your repository contains package.json files that are not properly locked.
Monorepos must have a root-level lockfile (package-lock.json, yarn.lock, or pnpm-lock.yaml).
Non-monorepos must ensure every package.json is locked in the same directory.

Rule: Repositories must use Docker base images from ECR
Description: Repositories must use Docker base images from the internal ECR repository, not from the public internet, as per [technology-stays-current/container-images](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/technology-stays-current/container-images/).

Rule: Repositories must use supported Docker images
Description:
Repositories must use official Skyscanner [Docker images](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/l/cp/CCfXdwKF) for all supported languages which include performance and security enhancements, as per
[technology-stays-current/container-images](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/technology-stays-current/container-images/).
Using stale or unsupported Docker images exposes Skyscanner travelers to security threats.

Please use official Skyscanner Docker images for all your projects. Review and act upon any open Dependabot pull requests against your repositories. If your project cannot receive dependency bumps by Dependabot, update your Docker images to the most recent version and reach out for support.

Failure Message:
Please remove your **active and publicly leaked** secrets from {{context.entity.descriptor.info.x-cortex-git.github.repository}} repository.

Rule: Components must follow custom metric naming conventions
Description: Components must follow custom metric naming conventions, as per [production-observable-and-resilient/metrics/01-instrumentation-and-transport](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/production-observable-and-resilient/metrics/01-instrumentation-and-transport).

Rule: Docker images must be immutable
Description:
Docker images pushed to ECR must not have a latest tag and must have immutable tags enabled, as per
[technology-stays-current/container-images](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/technology-stays-current/container-images/).
To comply with Security Tribe's [Project Digest](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/x/I3WnJ).
Failure Message:
You must set `latest-tag: false` and `immutable: true` on your Docker image.

You currently have `latest-tag` set to {{context.evaluation.captures.latest-tag}} and `immutable` set to {{context.evaluation.captures.immutable}}

Rule: Services must set maxRequestHeaderSize to 32KiB in production Dropwizard config
Description:
Microservices must explicitly set `maxRequestHeaderSize: 32KiB` in
`prod.yml` or equivalent production Dropwizard config, as per
[technology-stays-current/http-request-header-size](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/technology-stays-current/http-request-header-size/).

Rule: Teams must not have pending incidents older than 14 days
Description: Teams must not have incidents which are Mitigated but have not yet been closed that are older than 14 days. [See Live Incidents](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/production-observable-and-resilient/incident-management/live-incidents/)

Rule: Docker images must be immutable
Description:
Docker images pushed to ECR must not have a latest tag and must have immutable tags enabled, as per
[technology-stays-current/container-images](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/technology-stays-current/container-images/).
To comply with Security Tribe's [Project Digest](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/x/I3WnJ).

You must set `latest-tag: false` and `immutable: true` on your Docker image.

You currently have `latest-tag` set to {{context.evaluation.captures.latest-tag}} and `immutable` set to {{context.evaluation.captures.immutable}}

Rule: Projects must not use non-compliant cache engines
Description:
This rule is evaluated at the project level. Projects with caches must use Amazon ElastiCache
for Redis or Valkey. Memcached and non-Valkey ElastiCache Serverless deployments are not
permitted, as per
[technology-stays-current/caches-for-service-data](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/technology-stays-current/caches-for-service-data/).
Failure Message:
Your project has one or more caches using a non-compliant engine (Memcached or ElastiCache Serverless).
You should migrate to Amazon ElastiCache for Valkey as noted in the [caches-for-service-data](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/technology-stays-current/caches-for-service-data/) production standard.

To comply with Security Tribe's [Project Digest](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/x/I3WnJ).

Rule: Projects must not use non-compliant cache engines
Description:
This rule is evaluated at the project level. Projects with caches must use Amazon ElastiCache
for Redis or Valkey. Memcached and non-Valkey ElastiCache Serverless deployments are not
permitted, as per
[technology-stays-current/caches-for-service-data](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/technology-stays-current/caches-for-service-data/).
Failure Message:
Your project has one or more caches using a non-compliant engine (Memcached or ElastiCache Serverless).
You should migrate to Amazon ElastiCache for Valkey as noted in the [caches-for-service-data](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/technology-stays-current/caches-for-service-data/) production standard.

Please remediate the following CFRipper issues across all CloudFormation stacks in this project, which are considered particularly important by the Security team:
- BackupTagMissingInCriticalDataStoreRule
- CriticalTagMissingInOperationalDataStoreRule
- EC2SecurityGroupOpenToWorldRule
- ProjectTagInSelfServeRule
- ProjectTagPresentRule
- RestoreInCriticalDataStoreNonCompliantRule

You can view the specific findings in the [custom data section](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/SEC/pages/172832635/CFRipper+FAQ#Where-can-I-see-my-stack-issues-in-Cortex%3F).

Rule: Repositories must use Docker base images from ECR
Description: Repositories must use Docker base images from the internal ECR repository, not from the public internet, as per [technology-stays-current/container-images](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/technology-stays-current/container-images/).

Rule: Repositories must use supported Docker images
Description:
Repositories must use official Skyscanner [Docker images](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/l/cp/CCfXdwKF) for all supported languages which include performance and security enhancements, as per
[technology-stays-current/container-images](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/technology-stays-current/container-images/).
Using stale or unsupported Docker images exposes Skyscanner travelers to security threats.

Please use official Skyscanner Docker images for all your projects. Review and act upon any open Dependabot pull requests against your repositories. If your project cannot receive dependency bumps by Dependabot, update your Docker images to the most recent version and reach out for support.

Rule: Repositories must have all package.json files appropriately locked
Description: Repositories must have all package.json files properly locked to ensure reproducible builds, as per [changes-ship-reliably/reproducible-builds](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/changes-ship-reliably/reproducible-builds/).
Failure Message:
Your repository contains package.json files that are not properly locked.
Monorepos must have a root-level lockfile (package-lock.json, yarn.lock, or pnpm-lock.yaml).
Non-monorepos must ensure every package.json is locked in the same directory.

Rule: Repositories must have recent commits scanned by SonarQube
Description: Repositories must have less than 1 week between the most recent commit and most recent SonarQube scan to ensure code quality is continuously monitored, as per [anyone-can-contribute-safely/code-quality-monitoring](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/anyone-can-contribute-safely/code-quality-monitoring/).
Failure Message: Diff in weeks between most recent commit and most recent SonarQube scan: {{context.evaluation.captures.diff}}. [Note: a blank value indicates no scans performed]

Rule: Components must be deployed regularly
Description: Components must be redeployed at least once every four weeks to ensure redeployability for faster disaster recovery, as per [changes-ship-reliably/redeployability](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/changes-ship-reliably/redeployability/).
Failure Message: You have not redeployed your component in the past four weeks. Please ensure that it is redeployed regularly.

Rule: Projects must have no CFRipper selected issues across all stacks
Description: Projects must not have critical CFRipper issues related to backup tags, security groups, and project tags across any of their CloudFormation stacks, as per [customer-data-stays-protected/cloudformation-scanning](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/customer-data-stays-protected/cloudformation-scanning/).
Failure Message:
Please remediate the following CFRipper issues across all CloudFormation stacks in this project, which are considered particularly important by the Security team:
- BackupTagMissingInCriticalDataStoreRule
- CriticalTagMissingInOperationalDataStoreRule
- EC2SecurityGroupOpenToWorldRule
- ProjectTagInSelfServeRule
- ProjectTagPresentRule
- RestoreInCriticalDataStoreNonCompliantRule

You can view the specific findings in the [custom data section](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/SEC/pages/172832635/CFRipper+FAQ#Where-can-I-see-my-stack-issues-in-Cortex%3F).

If you believe an exception is required, please submit a request in #security-support with the [necessary information](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/SEC/pages/172832635/CFRipper+FAQ#How-can-I-get-an-exception-for-my-stack%3F).

More details about CFRipper can be found [here](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/SEC/pages/172832623/CFRipper).

Rule: Projects with CloudFormation stacks must have CFRipper data available
Description: Projects with CloudFormation stacks must have CFRipper security scanning data available, as per [customer-data-stays-protected/cloudformation-scanning](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/customer-data-stays-protected/cloudformation-scanning/).
Failure Message:
Please check the status of your CloudFormation stacks to ensure they are in a healthy state. Stacks in statuses like REVIEW_IN_PROGRESS or DELETE_FAILED may prevent template retrieval for security scanning, block resource updates, or lead to unexpected costs. If you believe your stacks should have been scanned and this does not apply to you, please contact Shield squad in #security-support.

More info about CFRipper can be found [here](https://skyscanner.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/SEC/pages/172832623/CFRipper).

Rule: Privacy and Consent Adoption
Description: Services are required to adopt at least the specified minimum versions of the listed libraries as part of the P0 Privacy & Consent remediation effort to [remove utid as per](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/technology-stays-current/consent-and-privacy-adoption/). This ensures that downstream data pipelines and ML models can transition from using utid to session_id.
Failure Message:
One or more P0 Privacy Change related libraries are below the minimum required version.
See the [Privacy and Consent: Session ID Adoption](https://production-standards.skyscannerpage.com/standards/technology-stays-current/consent-and-privacy-adoption/) 
page for context around this ask.
